original: “So, how did a kui-tan end up as a mercenary on Dhaal?” you ask. “Never been the best storyteller, but might as well give it a shot,” she starts. “So, my parents are both kui-tan from Jitafore and I lived there until my early thirties. It was a decent place. I got a job pretty early on, but nothing worth mentioning; my first proper job was working security. Nothing special, just keeping an eye on people and making sure that no one does anything against the rules. It was good work, although you wouldn’t believe how often I had to break up two or more people fucking in public spaces.” “That job didn’t last long. I didn’t quit or anything, but somehow got roped into working actual law enforcement. But I was already thinking of moving on that point. I didn’t hate the job, but that life just wasn’t for me, I wanted to see the rest of the galaxy. I could have joined up with the U.G.C., but I didn’t want some commanding officer to decide where I was going and what I was doing. So I spent a few years working off-jobs, sometimes mercenary, sometimes ship security,” Jana sighs. “After I met my boys — I can tell how it happened some time later if you’re interested — I started working a more stable mercenary job. Having a squad that you know and can trust makes a huge difference. We originally didn’t do much with any of the Rush planets, but a contact of mine got in touch with me, told how well the Zaibatsu pay. Made my way to the planet with my boys, and well, you know what happened then.” You clear your throat, feeling a little self-conscious from ruining her job. “Sounds like you had a good thing going. Sorry for busting your operation.” “It’s alright. It honestly was the wake-up call I needed. It’s not like I thought we were invincible, but being a merc, you’re living in constant danger. If it had been someone less generous than you, or an agent of one of the other Zaibatsu, my boys and I would have been screwed. Made into slaves or worse.” Jana flexes her cybernetic hand, thoughtfully. “So I got the hell out of there as soon as we could.” “So why a diner?” you ask, unable to help but to wonder why she went from mercenary work to running a restaurant. “We talked about what we were going to do after getting out of Dhaal safely. None of us wanted to work for someone else, and a couple of my boys already knew how to cook and behave, so after talking to a friend of mine he suggested that I could start a diner. At first, I wasn’t too convinced, but that same friend originally owned this place, and gave me a good deal on it. He’d shut it down a while back and hadn’t gotten a chance to sell it yet, so he was more than happy to get it off his back.” “How do you like the new job?” You ask. “I mean, I do miss the mercenary gigs. But I do like this line of work. Safe work. Ultimately, though, I care about getting to stick with my boys,” she finishes with a smile.

original: “How did you end up with a harem full of cat boys?” you ask. “Also, did you get all four of them at the same time? Because if not, it would be one hell of a coincidence.” “No, I didn’t end up with four kaithrit by coincidence,” she responds, laughing a little. “I met them while I was working as a merc. I had been hired to do some pseudo law enforcement. The employer had some trouble with break ins, and they hired me to deal with it. Turns out that my boys had been up to it. Didn’t take much to get them to behave — they weren’t much of fighters back then. Even though I probably should have turned them in, I wasn’t hired to arrest them, just to take care of the break in problem. So instead of letting the UGC have them, I took all four under my wing.” “How did they feel about it at the start?” you ask. They might be willing bottoms now, but you could definitely imagine them as more prickly when they first met. “Well, they weren’t hopping on my dick from the get-go,” Jana laughs. “Not that it even was a consideration back then. I offered them an ultimatum; either I would take them under my wing, or I would turn them over to the proper authorities. They were more than happy to not have to go to prison, and on top of that, they’d be getting training as well.” She adjusts in her seat. “Them being turned into a part of my harem wasn’t a part of the deal, but when I was training them, they decided to start teasing me. I was standing behind one of them, helping with his shooting stance, and he decided to be a brat. It didn’t take long for him to get me hard — that fat ass grinding against me was just too much. It didn’t take long after that for them to get to an acceptable level of skill.” “That’s certainly one way of handling the carrot and stick approach,” you joke. “So did you take them onto your mercenary work after that?” “I did, but I went it slow at first to make sure everything went smoothly. No amount of training will prepare you for the real thing. But they took to it well, and I didn’t need to baby them. I’m glad that they could work on their own. I don’t mind providing for my boys, given the relationship we have... but at the same time, I don’t want them to be left with no skills to continue their lives with if something were to happen.” “Makes sense to set them up for independence. You can’t see the future,” you muse. As the conversation winds down, you think of what to talk to her next.
